summary: License limit enforcement testing for multi-tenant SaaS. Confirmed 2 CRITICAL backend bugs: /admin/orders/manual and /admin/subscriptions/manual do NOT call check_limit or increment_monthly — orders/subscriptions are created even when at monthly limit. Fixed 1 MongoDB bug in increment_monthly (WriteError code 40). Frontend LimitBanner, Usage & Limits page, and platform admin license modal all working correctly.

critical.issue: CONFIRMED BUG: Missing check_limit + increment_monthly calls. Returns 200 (order created) when orders_count=1 and max_orders_per_month=1. Should return 403. File: backend/routes/admin/orders.py, create_manual_order() function.
critical.fix_guidance: Add before order creation: check = await check_limit(tenant_id, 'orders'); if not check['allowed']: raise HTTPException(403, ...). Add after order creation: await increment_monthly(tenant_id, 'orders'). Reference: checkout.py lines 153-167.

critical.issue: CONFIRMED BUG: Missing check_limit + increment_monthly calls. Returns 200 (subscription created) when subscriptions_count=1 and max_subscriptions_per_month=1. Should return 403. File: backend/routes/admin/subscriptions.py, create_manual_subscription() function.
critical.fix_guidance: Same pattern as above: add check_limit before creation and increment_monthly after. Reference: checkout.py lines 153-167.

minor.issue: FIXED: MongoDB WriteError code 40 (ConflictingUpdateOperators) — $inc and $setOnInsert both referenced the same counter field path. Fix applied: removed incremented field from $setOnInsert block. File: backend/services/license_service.py

integration_issues.flow: Create Manual Order when at limit
integration_issues.issue: The Create Manual Order modal opens and submits without showing a 403 error because the backend does not enforce the limit. The LimitBanner warns but the modal is not proactively blocked. This is a consequence of the backend bug above.

action_items: E1 MUST FIX: Add check_limit('orders') BEFORE order creation in routes/admin/orders.py create_manual_order() — return 403 when blocked
E1 MUST FIX: Add increment_monthly('orders') AFTER order creation in routes/admin/orders.py create_manual_order()
E1 MUST FIX: Add check_limit('subscriptions') BEFORE subscription creation in routes/admin/subscriptions.py create_manual_subscription() — return 403 when blocked
E1 MUST FIX: Add increment_monthly('subscriptions') AFTER subscription creation in routes/admin/subscriptions.py create_manual_subscription()
Reference pattern in checkout.py lines 153-167 and 282-284
increment_monthly MongoDB bug already fixed by T1 in license_service.py
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: routes/admin/orders.py create_manual_order() — no check_limit or increment_monthly call. checkout.py has the correct pattern at lines 153-167.
CRITICAL: routes/admin/subscriptions.py create_manual_subscription() — same missing calls.

FIXED: license_service.py increment_monthly() — had MongoDB ConflictingUpdateOperators error (code 40) because the same counter field path appeared in both $inc and $setOnInsert. Fixed by excluding the incremented field from $setOnInsert.
NOTE: Usage & Limits page also shows Admin Users: 4/3 Blocked for bright-accounting. This may be pre-existing or from test user creation. E1 should verify if this is expected.

success_rate.backend: 90% (18 passed, 2 failed as expected KNOWN BUGS, 1 skipped - GoCardless not enabled)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (LimitBanner, Usage & Limits page, platform admin license modal all working)

seed_data_creation: Set license limits for bright-accounting (max_orders=1, max_subs=1) and orders_count=1 in license_usage collection. These were cleaned up by test teardown (limits reset to null, counters reset to 0).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two critical backend bugs confirmed: (1) /admin/orders/manual and (2) /admin/subscriptions/manual both MISSING check_limit + increment_monthly calls. Frontend LimitBanner, Usage & Limits page, and platform admin license modal confirmed working. The increment_monthly MongoDB bug was fixed by T1 in license_service.py. After E1 fixes the two backend bugs, run test_iter149_license_limits.py again and the two currently-failing tests should PASS (returning 403).

bug_1_and_2.description: check_limit + increment_monthly not called in manual order/subscription creation
bug_1_and_2.root_cause: The checkout.py bank-transfer endpoint has the correct pattern (lines 153-167 and 282-284). But routes/admin/orders.py create_manual_order() and routes/admin/subscriptions.py create_manual_subscription() were implemented without adding the same license enforcement pattern.
bug_1_and_2.reproduction: 1. Set max_orders_per_month=1 via PUT /api/admin/tenants/{id}/license. 2. Set orders_count=1 in license_usage (MongoDB). 3. POST /api/admin/orders/manual as partner admin → returns 200 instead of 403.
bug_1_and_2.mitigation: Add check_limit before creation and increment_monthly after creation in both endpoints. See checkout.py for reference.

bug_3_fixed.root_cause: increment_monthly used $inc {customers_count: 1} AND $setOnInsert {customers_count: 1} in the same update_one operation. MongoDB rejects this as ConflictingUpdateOperators (code 40) regardless of whether document exists or is being inserted.
bug_3_fixed.fix_applied: Removed the incremented field from $setOnInsert block. $inc handles initial value correctly on upsert (treats missing field as 0).
